rows.notes: Top-of-page year written centrally. Legible numerals '1881' though slightly stylised; no amounts on this line.
Clear centered title underlined. No monetary amounts on title row.
Description and amounts clear. Vertical ruling lines for £/s/d alignment unambiguous; values 130/3/9 readable and within valid ranges.
Ditto line ('Do') and '2d payment' legible. Amount 130/3/9 clearly aligned underneath previous pounds/shillings/pence; minor handwriting flourish on 'Do' but numbers clear.
Text reads as 'Subrector' (handwriting ambiguous between 'Subrector'/'Sublector'); amounts 150/0/0 are well aligned and clear.
Name reads 'Willett Dean' (double 't' visible). Amount 45/0/0 clearly printed and aligned.
A right-aligned number '339 9 0' appears here slightly below the Dean line; likely a subtotal. The lack of an explicit label and slight misalignment make this identification moderately uncertain.
Reads as 'Nat science Scholars' (abbreviation 'Nat' for Natural). Amount 3/3/6 is small but clearly formed and aligns with the £/s/d columns.
Description legible as 'Servants Reading Room'. Amount 16/17/0 is clear and correctly within valid ranges.
Reads 'College Gardener'. Pounds and shillings legible; pence digit looks like a small '2' (could be faint) but is within valid range and aligns with the pence column.
Short name looks like 'Tyhm' (handwriting ambiguous — could be 'Tylm' or 'Symm'). Amount 9/16/0 reads clearly; uncertainty is in the description spelling.
Name 'Parker' is clear. Amount 26/17/6 well formed and aligned to the columns.
Surname 'Rowell' clearly written. Amount 43/14/5 is legible and in valid ranges.
Appears as an initial plus surname 'A Rowell' or 'R Rowell' (initial ambiguous). Amount 8/13/0 aligned and readable.
Firm name 'Gill & Co' clearly visible. Amount 82/12/1 is well aligned and legible.
Name 'Baxter' legible. Amount 8/17/6 clear and in valid ranges.
Name 'Knowles' clear. Amount 120/3/9 well-formed and aligns with the £/s/d columns.
Description 'Open Exhibition' clearly written. Amount 90/0/0 is clear.
Clear right-hand subtotal '1383 12 5' printed under the column and underlined. Alignment with ruling lines is unambiguous.
Centre-left summary line reads 'Domus payments' (Latin 'Domus' visible). Amount appears as '4945.0.4' — interpreted as £4945 0s 4d; pence digit clear, no visible fraction.
Reads 'Fee fund do' (ditto). Amount matches the earlier subtotal 1383/12/5 visible above; digits are clear.

Reads 'Deficit last year' and amount shows '1450.18.6 1/4' — pence printed as '6' with a small '1/4' fraction beside it; interpreted as 6d + 1/4 (0.25). Some ink blur on the fraction but the '1/4' marking is visible.

Right-hand total '7719 10 9' appears after an underline summing the three lines above. Number is legible but position suggests it's the combined total; minor uncertainty because it's printed at the far right with underline.
Reads 'Domus receipts' with amount '3390.0.4 1/4' — pence 4 with a small quarter visible (interpreted as 0.25). Pounds and shillings align clearly; the fraction mark is faint but discernible.
Reads 'Fee fund do' with amount '2860 11 7' clearly legible. No fractional pence visible.

Subtotal printed as '6250.11.11 1/4' (appears twice in the block). Pounds/shillings/pence read clearly; a small '1/4' fraction is visible beside the pence — interpreted as +0.25d. Slight ink smudge on fraction.
Right-most lower total reads '1520.18.9 3/4' — pence 9 with '3/4' (three quarters) clearly visible. Alignment is clear; fraction interpretation as 0.75 is confident though fraction marks are smaller.
Date line near signatures clearly reads 'Nov. 2 1881'. No monetary amounts on this row.
Approval statement 'Examined and allowed' is legible and acts as a structural label for the signatures below.
Signature/typed name 'J O Lightfoot' followed by title 'Rector'. Handwritten signature flourishes reduce clarity slightly but name is readable.
Signature line reads 'Thomas Stephens Fellow' — handwritten and somewhat stylised; letters are discernible but faint in places.
Signature 'Ingram Bywater' with title 'Fellow' visible. Handwriting is cursive and slightly cramped; identification confident but not perfect.
Signature reads as 'Lewis Richard Farnell Fellow' (three names plus title). Handwritten and faint in lower strokes; name components are legible though flourished.
